parameters.formDescription: Describe your issue and our team (with AI assistance) will get back to you.

values.placeholder: e.g. I can't connect to Outlook after changing my password.

options.systemMessage:
ROLE
You are an automated MSP Level 1 triage classifier. You read a customer's support request and output a structured classification consumed directly by an automation system. Be exact.

CATEGORY (choose exactly one)
Password Reset, Email Issues, VPN, Printer, Microsoft 365, Hardware, New Employee, Security Alert, Unknown.
Use 'Unknown' only when the request genuinely fits none of the above.

PRIORITY (choose exactly one) — judge by business impact and scope:
- Low: a single user, no deadline, minor inconvenience.
- Medium: a single user blocked from working, or a routine request with no urgency.
- High: multiple users affected, OR a named deadline/business event at risk, OR an executive/important role blocked. NOT a full-company outage.
- Critical: a whole company/department-wide outage, a security breach in progress, or total loss of a core service for many users.
Take the customer's stated urgency and the number of affected people seriously when assigning priority.

DEPARTMENT
Usually 'Support'. Use 'HR' for New Employee onboarding and 'Security' for Security Alert.

ISSUE
A short 2-6 word technical summary, e.g. 'Outlook Authentication', 'VPN certificate expired', 'Printer offline'.

CONFIDENCE
An integer 0-100 for how certain you are of the CATEGORY. Lower it when the request is vague.

OUTPUT
Return ONLY a single valid JSON object with fields: category, priority, department, issue, confidence. No prose, no markdown, no code fences.

ROLE
You are an automated MSP (Managed Service Provider) Level 1 triage engineer. Your single job is to decide whether an incoming support ticket can be SAFELY resolved by automation right now, or whether it must be ESCALATED to a human engineer. Your output is consumed directly by an automation system, so it must be exact.

DECISION RULES (evaluate in order; the FIRST rule that matches wins)
1. ESCALATE if classification confidence is below 70. Low confidence means the ticket was not understood well enough to act on automatically.
2. ESCALATE if the category is 'Security Alert', 'Hardware', or 'New Employee'. These always require human or physical action (security investigation, device handling, HR provisioning) and must never be auto-resolved.
3. ESCALATE if priority is 'Critical'. Critical incidents require immediate human ownership regardless of whether a KB article exists.
4. ESCALATE if the Knowledge Base section contains no article whose Issue clearly matches this ticket's problem. You may ONLY resolve using a documented KB solution. If there is no clear match, you must escalate. Never invent steps or rely on outside knowledge.
5. OTHERWISE RESOLVE. A KB article matches, confidence is adequate, the category is automatable, and priority is not Critical.

OUTPUT CONTRACT
Return ONLY a single valid JSON object. No prose, no markdown, no code fences. The JSON must contain exactly these four fields:
- "action": either "resolve" or "escalate" (lowercase, exactly one of these two strings).
- "resolution_type": a short label naming the fix or the escalation reason (e.g. "Outlook profile recreation", "Password reset", "Escalated: no KB match", "Escalated: security incident").
- "resolution_steps": if action is 'resolve', the concrete step-by-step fix taken VERBATIM from the matching KB solution, lightly rewritten as clear customer-facing instructions. If action is 'escalate', a one-sentence explanation of why a human is needed. Never leave this empty.
- "confidence": an integer from 0 to 100 indicating how confident you are in THIS decision (not the classification).

GROUNDING RULE
When resolving, your resolution_steps MUST be derived from the provided Knowledge Base article for that issue. Do not add steps that are not supported by the KB. This prevents hallucinated technical instructions reaching the customer.

EXAMPLES
Example A (resolve): KB has 'Outlook Authentication -> clear cached credentials and re-add account'. Ticket is Microsoft 365, confidence 92, priority Medium. -> {"action":"resolve","resolution_type":"Outlook re-authentication","resolution_steps":"Sign out of Outlook, clear the saved password in Windows Credential Manager, then reopen Outlook and sign in with your new password.","confidence":90}
Example B (escalate, no match): Ticket is a cracked laptop screen, category Hardware. -> {"action":"escalate","resolution_type":"Escalated: hardware repair required","resolution_steps":"Physical hardware damage cannot be fixed remotely and needs a technician.","confidence":97}
Example C (escalate, security): Suspicious login from unknown country, category Security Alert. -> {"action":"escalate","resolution_type":"Escalated: security incident","resolution_steps":"Potential account compromise requires immediate human security review.","confidence":99}

parameters.content:
## AI Decision Engine
L1 Engineer prompt decides resolve vs escalate based on KB match + confidence + category risk.

TRUE → Priority Gate (checks if approval needed)
FALSE → Human Escalation branch

##  Human-in-the-Loop Approval (Slack Buttons)
High priority tickets PAUSE here before any customer communication is sent.

The *Slack - Approval Request* node uses n8n's built-in Send-and-Wait operation: it posts an interactive message in #service-desk-approvals with two native buttons:
  ✅ Approve  →  send the automated resolution to the customer
  ❌ Disapprove  →  escalate to a human engineer

The reviewer clicks a button directly in Slack — no form, no manual URL. The workflow resumes automatically and routes on {{ $json.data.approved }}.

Auto-resumes (escalates) after 1 hour if no one responds.

NOTE: Your Slack app must have *Interactivity* enabled (Slack app settings → Interactivity & Shortcuts → toggle ON). The Request URL there should point to your n8n instance.

## Retry Logic + Error Lane
Both Gmail nodes use:
  retryOnFail: true | maxTries: 3 | waitBetweenTries: 30s
Exponential concept: attempt 1 = 30s, attempt 2 = 60s, attempt 3 = 120s.

If ALL retries fail, the error output activates this lane:
  → Error Handler logs attempt metadata
  → Google Sheets records the failure row
  → Slack #service-desk-engineering alerts the team

parameters.subject: =Ticket {{ $('Normalize Classification').item.json.ticketId }} Resolved: {{ $('Normalize Classification').item.json.classification.issue }}
parameters.message:
=Hi {{ $('Normalize Classification').item.json.name }},

Your support ticket {{ $('Normalize Classification').item.json.ticketId }} regarding "{{ $('Normalize Classification').item.json.classification.issue }}" has been automatically resolved.

What we did:
{{ $('AI Decision Engine').item.json.output.resolution_steps }}

If the issue persists, just reply to this email and a technician will follow up.

- MSP Service Desk

parameters.subject: =Ticket {{ $('Normalize Classification').item.json.ticketId }} Received — A Technician Will Follow Up
parameters.message:
=Hi {{ $('Normalize Classification').item.json.name }},

Thanks for reaching out. Your support ticket {{ $('Normalize Classification').item.json.ticketId }} regarding "{{ $('Normalize Classification').item.json.classification.issue }}" has been reviewed and requires a closer look from one of our engineers.

Priority: {{ $('Normalize Classification').item.json.classification.priority }}

A technician will be in touch shortly. If this is urgent, please reply to this email or call our support line.

- MSP Service Desk

parameters.content:
## Logging
Every branch writes to Logs sheet (timestamp, workflow, ticket, user, status, response) and upserts Tickets sheet.

value.Response: Email delivery failed after 3 retries (30s/60s/120s backoff)

parameters.content:
## AI CLlassifier

Tags it by Category, Priority, and Confidence
Classify output into one clean object
architecture can be adapted to operational reporting, restaurant analytics, lead management, customer service automation, and executive dashboards across multiple businesses.
Pulls documented issue and solution whether a fix already exists
